channel.title: ML on PostDoc Problems
channel.description: Recent content in ML on PostDoc Problems

item.title: llmstep and LeanInfer
item.description:
I came across two LLM based tactics for Lean

  • llmstep: [L]LM proofstep suggestions in Lean
  • LeanInfer: Neural Network Inference in Lean 4

They both use [LeanDojo](2023-09-04 leandojo.qmd) somehow and I don’t understand the difference between the two. Unfortunately, I do not know enough about LLM and AI to tell if this is actually good or just hot gas.

item.title: LeanDojo
item.description:
This was posted a while back on the Lean Zulip:

LeanDojo

LeanDojo: Theorem Proving with Retrieval-Augmented Language Models

According to their description, LeanDojo is an open-source Lean playground consisting of toolkits, data, models, and benchmarks. Plus they also provide an LLM-based prover and a chatGPT plugin. All of this looks amazing and something worth learning more about.
